data.content:
# Implementation Plan: Shared Interactive Agent Session Runtime (v4)

## Prior Review Feedback Addressed

Previous plan rejected for: (1) Gemini missing from AgentDialect/capabilities/tests, (2) capability shape lacked explicit turn-result detection, session-end detection, and post-exit validation hooks. This revision addresses both.

## Architecture

### New file: agent-session-capabilities.ts (~150 lines)

Central capability registry with full session runtime descriptor per dialect:

```typescript
interface TurnResultDetector {
  resultEventTypes: readonly string[];
  accumulatesText: boolean;
  extractResult(event: Record<string, unknown>): {
    result: string; isError: boolean;
  } | null;
}

interface SessionEndDetector {
  mode: "stdin-eof" | "process-exit";
  expectCleanExitOnStdinClose: boolean;
}

type PostExitValidator = (
  exitCode: number,
  lastEvents: ReadonlyArray<Record<string, unknown>>,
) => { valid: boolean; reason?: string };

interface StdinDrainPolicy {
  closeOnResult: boolean;
  graceMs: number;
}

interface WatchdogPolicy {
  inactivityTimeoutMs: number;
  timeoutSignal: NodeJS.Signals;
}

interface AgentSessionCapabilities {
  interactive: boolean;
  promptTransport: "stdin" | "cli-arg";
  followUpSupport: boolean;
  autoAnswerAskUser: boolean;
  turnResult: TurnResultDetector;
  sessionEnd: SessionEndDetector;
  stdinDrain: StdinDrainPolicy;
  watchdog: WatchdogPolicy;
  postExitValidation: PostExitValidator | null;
}
```

### Per-Dialect Capability Matrix

| Capability | Claude | Codex | Copilot | OpenCode | Gemini |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| interactive | true | false | false | false | false |
| promptTransport | stdin | cli-arg | cli-arg | cli-arg | cli-arg |
| followUpSupport | true | false | false | false | false |
| autoAnswerAskUser | true | false | true | false | false |
| turnResult.resultEventTypes | ["result"] | ["result"] | ["result"] | ["result"] | ["result"] |
| turnResult.accumulatesText | false | true | true | true | true |
| sessionEnd.mode | stdin-eof | process-exit | process-exit | process-exit | process-exit |
| stdinDrain.closeOnResult | true | false | false | false | false |
| stdinDrain.graceMs | 2000 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| watchdog.inactivityTimeoutMs | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| postExitValidation | null | codexPostExit* | null | null | null |

*codexPostExit: validates last event was turn.completed (not turn.failed) when exit code 0.

All normalizers already convert dialect-specific events to {type: "result"}, so extractResult works uniformly on the normalized stream. Callers use extractResult instead of ad-hoc obj.type === "result" checks.

### Gemini dialect addition in agent-adapter.ts

- Add "gemini" to AgentDialect union (line 16-20)
- Add gemini detection in resolveDialect() (before default return)
- Add buildPromptModeArgs() branch for gemini (one-shot like Codex)
- Add createGeminiNormalizer() stub (exact JSONL format TBD)
- Wire into createLineNormalizer()

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Create agent-session-capabilities.ts (~150 lines new)
- All interfaces + getSessionCapabilities(dialect) function
- Capability map for all 5 dialects encoding current behavior exactly
- Centralized killProcessGroup(pid) and scheduleForceKill(pid, delayMs) helpers
- Dependencies: None | Risk: Low

### Step 2: Add Gemini to agent-adapter.ts (~40 lines changed)
- Add to AgentDialect, resolveDialect(), buildPromptModeArgs(), createLineNormalizer()
- Provisional normalizer marked TODO
- Dependencies: None (parallel with Step 1) | Risk: Low

### Step 3: Refactor initial path to use capabilities (~30 lines in 2 files)
Files: terminal-manager-initial-child.ts, terminal-manager-initial-io.ts
- Replace isInteractive = dialect === "claude" (line 90) with caps.interactive
- Pass caps to createInitialChildState(); use caps.stdinDrain.graceMs
- Gate resultFollowUp() on caps.followUpSupport
- Gate autoAnswerAskUser() on caps.autoAnswerAskUser
- Use caps.turnResult.extractResult() instead of obj.type === "result"
- Wire caps.postExitValidation in wireClose()
- Dependencies: Step 1 | Risk: Medium

### Step 4: Refactor take-loop path (~25 lines)
File: terminal-manager-take-child.ts
- Same as Step 3: replace dialect === "claude" (line 62) with caps
- Use caps for all gating and extractResult calls
- Wire caps.postExitValidation in wireClose()
- Dependencies: Step 1 | Risk: Medium

### Step 5: Centralize process-group kill (~15 lines)
File: terminal-manager.ts - replace inline kill in abortSession() with centralized helpers
- Dependencies: Step 1 | Risk: Low

### Step 6: Conformance tests (~350 lines new, 2 new + 1 modified)
- agent-session-capabilities.test.ts (~200 lines): parameterized across all 5 dialects
- agent-adapter-gemini.test.ts (~80 lines): resolution, args, normalizer
- agent-adapter.test.ts (~30 lines): add Gemini to matrix
- Dependencies: Steps 1-2 | Risk: Low

### Step 7: Update existing tests (~20 lines across ~3 files)
- Update mocks referencing dialect === "claude" to use capabilities
- Dependencies: Steps 3-5 | Risk: Low

## Dependency Graph
Steps 1+2 parallel -> Steps 3,4,5 parallel (after 1) -> Step 7; Step 6 after 1+2

## Complexity: Medium
New: ~190 lines | Modified: ~90 lines | Tests: ~350 lines | Net: ~630 lines | 3 new files, 5 modified

## Risks
1. Gemini CLI format unknown -> stub normalizer (Medium)
2. Breaking initial-path follow-up -> existing tests validate (Medium)
3. agent-adapter.ts near 500 lines -> capabilities in separate file (Medium)
4. Circular imports -> new module has no upward deps (Low)

## Acceptance Criteria Mapping
1. Shared interface -> Step 1
2. Terminal-manager uses shared abstraction -> Steps 3, 4
3. Centralizes watchdog/kill/close-input/follow-up -> Steps 1, 5
4. Conformance tests -> Steps 6, 7
